lint.note: Pre-existing lint failures: golangci-lint binary (go1.24) incompatible with go1.26 stdlib. All errors in third-party modules (sentry-go, otel, grpc). Identical to round 1 lint output. Not related to docs-cleanup changes.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated architecture.md health endpoint section to document exact field names and values: config returns {status, hasUsername, hasPassword, hasBaseUrl}, upstream returns {status: 'reachable'|'unreachable'} with no error field, ffmpeg returns {status: 'available'|'not_found'} with no version field.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a note in the documentation section about the meta.mode distinction: API server returns mode: 'api', CLI --json returns mode: 'json'. Workers updating docs should check which surface they are documenting.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: update-all-docs review found all API docs incorrectly using mode: 'json'. The mode distinction is documented in architecture.md but not in AGENTS.md guidance for doc writers.

rejectedObservations.observation: docs/observability.md references config options and env vars that don't exist in the codebase: metricsEndpoint, IMPARTUS_VERBOSE (also with typo 'IMpartus_VERBOSE').
rejectedObservations.reason: Pre-existing documentation accuracy issue. Not a shared state observation suitable for library or services.yaml. The orchestrator can decide whether to create a follow-up task for observability.md cleanup.

rejectedObservations.observation: docs/observability.md references 8 IMPARTUS_FLAG_* environment variables that no longer work after feature flag removal.
rejectedObservations.reason: Already rejected in round 1. The fix-docs-accuracy feature addressed this by removing these references. The remaining non-existent env vars (IMPARTUS_VERBOSE, metricsEndpoint) are a separate pre-existing issue.

rejectedObservations.observation: go-backend-worker SKILL.md references deleted feature_flags.go file.
rejectedObservations.reason: Already addressed by a prior commit (e40c722: 'chore: remove stale feature_flags.go reference from worker skill').
